ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for Tauhu Sumbat (stuffed fried tofu with vegetables). Closest match is fried tofu food product (FOODON_03315671); the stuffed-with-vegetables aspect is not captured.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP covers fried tofu generically; the stuffed vegetable filling is not represented in the code hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No applicable SNOMED CT food concept identified for Tauhu Sumbat.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Tauhu Sumbat is approximately 120 g, consisting of two to three deep-fried tofu pockets stuffed with shredded cucumber, bean sprouts, and carrot, served with a small portion of peanut sauce or chili dip.

nutrient_source.title: Fried tofu (firm) and mixed raw vegetables — composite estimate
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (SR Legacy / Foundation Foods) and Singapore HPB Foodo database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for Tauhu Sumbat as a composite dish. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for fried firm tofu (FDC ID 172443) and HPB Singapore Foodo data for similar tofu-based snacks. The input AI-generated values (confidence 0.9) are broadly consistent with these approved sources and have been cross-checked and retained where they align. Saturated fat estimated at approximately 2.0 g/100 g based on USDA fried tofu fatty acid profile. Monounsaturated and polyunsaturated fat set to -1 as detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite dish is not available in approved sources. Cholesterol set to -1 as tofu is plant-based and cholesterol data for this composite is not separately reported. Water_g set to -1 as moisture data for this composite preparation is not available in approved sources.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Soy-Based Foods — analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database entries for tofu and mixed vegetable dishes
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for Tauhu Sumbat in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 30 is estimated by analogy with firm tofu (GI ~15) and mixed vegetable dishes (GI ~30–40) from the University of Sydney GI Database. The high protein and fibre content of tofu and raw vegetables supports a low GI classification. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (30 × 10.2) / 100 ≈ 3.1 (carbohydrate per 120 g serving = 8.5 × 1.2 = 10.2 g).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (210 mg/100 g) and additional sodium from peanut sauce or chili dip
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should consume Tauhu Sumbat in moderation and request low-sodium dipping sauces, as the combined sodium from the fried tofu and accompanying sauces can contribute meaningfully to daily sodium limits of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~30) driven by high soy protein and dietary fibre from raw vegetable filling
type2_diabetes.guidance: Tauhu Sumbat has a low estimated glycemic index of approximately 30 and a glycemic load of approximately 3 per 120 g serving, making it a suitable snack option for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed without high-sugar dipping sauces.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Deep-frying increases total fat (12.5 g/100 g) including saturated fat; soy protein is cardioprotective
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The deep-frying process elevates total and saturated fat content, which warrants moderation for individuals with cardiovascular disease; however, the soy protein base may offer a modest cardioprotective benefit, and portion control with avoidance of high-sodium sauces is advised.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Tauhu Sumbat is a popular Malay snack and side dish found throughout Malaysia and Singapore. It consists of blocks of firm tofu that are deep-fried until golden and crispy, then slit open to form a pocket and stuffed with a mixture of fresh, raw, shredded vegetables — typically cucumber, bean sprouts, and carrot. The dish is served with a spicy peanut sauce or a sweet-and-spicy chili dip. The eating experience offers a satisfying contrast between the warm, crispy exterior of the fried tofu and the cool, crunchy, refreshing vegetable filling. Nutritionally, Tauhu Sumbat provides approximately 185 kcal per 100 g, with 9.2 g of protein from soy, 12.5 g of fat from deep-frying, and 8.5 g of carbohydrates. It is a good source of calcium (150 mg/100 g) and iron (1.8 mg/100 g) from the tofu. The dish has a low estimated glycemic index of approximately 30, making it suitable for individuals managing blood sugar. Sodium content of 210 mg/100 g, which can increase significantly with dipping sauces, warrants attention for those managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. Tauhu Sumbat is vegetarian, vegan, and halal, and is commonly consumed as a breakfast, lunch, dinner side, or snack across Malay-speaking communities in Southeast Asia.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Tauhu Sumbat?
Is Tauhu Sumbat suitable for diabetics?
What is stuffed tofu in Malay?
What vegetables are in Tauhu Sumbat?
Is Tauhu Sumbat high in sodium?
What sauce is served with Tauhu Sumbat?

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for Tauhu Sumbat as a composite dish. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy fried firm tofu data and HPB Singapore Foodo comparable tofu snack entries. The AI-generated input values are broadly consistent with these approved sources. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database entries for tofu and mixed vegetable dishes. Confidence is medium rather than high due to the composite nature of the dish and the absence of an exact approved-source match.
notes: 1. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 (confirmed zero): Tauhu Sumbat is a plant-based dish with no animal-derived ingredients; vitamin B12 is naturally zero for this food. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 (confirmed zero): tofu and raw vegetables contain negligible vitamin D; this is consistent with USDA FoodData Central data for fried firm tofu. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown, cholesterol, and moisture data for this composite Tauhu Sumbat preparation are not available as a single entry in USDA FoodData Central or the Singapore HPB Foodo database as of April 2026. 3. Saturated fat estimated at 2.0 g/100 g based on USDA SR Legacy fatty acid profile for fried firm tofu (FDC ID 172443). 4. GI of 30 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database entries for tofu and mixed vegetable dishes; no direct GI measurement for Tauhu Sumbat exists in the database. 5. Glycemic load per serving (120 g) calculated as (30 × 10.2) / 100 ≈ 3.1. 6. The input source 'Malaysian Dietitians Association' is not on the approved source list and has not been used as primary evidence; all nutrient and GI values have been cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central and HPB Singapore Foodo. 7. Ontology: FoodOn FOODON_03315671 and FoodEx2 A0FKP are approximate matches for fried tofu; the stuffed vegetable filling aspect is not captured in either code hierarchy.
